feature: Mongolia correction phase 1 — clear the two HARD verifier failures
requirement: Correct the two US-market assumptions that verify-docs.sh currently fails on: DEC-6's two-part name model (16 occurrences across 5 documents), and the FDIC/NCUA sponsor-bank framing in 04 section 1.2. Scope is deliberately limited to these two; currency redenomination, rails mapping, vendor removal and KPI re-baselining are seeded into the backlog for a later run because 03_acceptance_criteria.md alone carries 216 monetary values and would exhaust a worker's context.
backlog: DEC-18 currency: USD -> MNT. 312 monetary values across 6 documents; 03_acceptance_criteria.md holds 216 of them. Needs a redenomination policy first (spot-rate conversion vs re-derivation from Mongolian incomes) because most are thresholds, not illustrations.

Payment rails: 56 ACH/FedNow/SEPA references -> Banksuljee (RTGS, above MNT 5,000,000) / ACH+ (at or below) / NETC for cards. The threshold is set by Governor's order, so it must land as configuration, not a literal.

Vendors: Stripe, Plaid, Lithic, Persona. Note these are leaked into schema field names (persona_inquiry_id, plaid_item_ref) and API error codes (502 PLAID_UNAVAILABLE), so removal is a requirements change, not an integration change.
KPI re-baselining: KPI-1.3 targets 100,000 members against a total Mongolian SCC sector of 78,608; KPI-2.1 targets AUM roughly 1.5x the entire sector's assets.
Rate model: DEC-48's 12% APR base sits below the 17.45% bank-funded market rate, and DEC-49's 6% floor is the subsidised state mortgage rate. Both sides of the spread are inverted.

00_market_research.md is entirely US/EU-framed — competitor set is Alliant, Desjardins, Coop Pank, Monzo; segments are Gen Z ESG consumers and W-2-adjacent gig workers. Needs rewriting against the Mongolian market, not editing.

T6 first attempt could not corroborate the claim that a Law on Financial Cooperatives passed the Economic Standing Committee on 2026-04-07 — a claim the ORCHESTRATOR supplied in the task prompt. Treat as [UNVERIFIED] until a primary source is opened.

Plan said the FDIC/NCUA text sits in 04 section 1.2; T6 verified it is in section 1.1 System Context. Orchestrator error — check section anchors against the document before writing them into a task description.

verify-docs.sh blind spot: the first_name/last_name HARD check matches only the snake_case tokens, so the same Western name model expressed in prose ('embossed_name from first/last name' at 04:829, 04:1087) passes silently. Add a DRIFT counter for the prose form.

verify-docs.sh n_snake_name uses grep -rEoh with no context exclusion, unlike the money-type check which excludes 'minor units|rejected|superseded'. The tokens are therefore banned even inside 'Superseded Variants' columns documenting what the old drafts said. Decide whether that is intended.

DEC-35 (05:159, status CONFIRM) and 03:476 specify P2P recipient confirmation as 'first name + last initial'. Under the ratified three-part model there is no last name to initial — etsgiin_ner is a patronymic and ner is the identity, so the display inverts. Found by T4 outside the migration map; deliberately NOT fixed because it changes a ratified decision in the DEC-3 payee-addressing territory that DEC-6(c) carves out. Needs its own ratification decision. Gate unaffected — no forbidden token.

Migration map item [1] writes 'etsgiin ner' with a space where every other item uses 'etsgiin_ner'. Applied verbatim per instruction and flagged rather than silently normalised. Cosmetic, but decide the canonical spelling before it propagates.

tasks.title: Draft DEC-6 replacement: three-part Mongolian name model
tasks.description:
Draft replacement text for DEC-6 in 01_business_analysis.md, plus a migration map of every affected occurrence. Do NOT edit any document — produce the draft in your handoff only.

The model must be three fields, not two: ovog (clan name, optional, near-zero discriminating power since most families registered 'Borjigin'), etsgiin ner (patronymic — father's given name in genitive, placed BEFORE the given name), and ner (the given name, which is the person's actual identity and the sort key).

Must specify: (a) that the patronymic occupies the surname slot in passport MRZ, so siblings share a surname but a parent and child never do — any 'same surname = same household' heuristic is wrong; (b) Cyrillic stored as canonical with the MRZ Latin string kept verbatim separately; (c) matching on registration number, never on name, because Cyrillic-to-Latin transliteration has no enforced standard.

Catalogue the 16 occurrences: 01_business_analysis.md (3, including DEC-6 itself at ~line 362 and the glossary row at ~line 389), 02_user_stories.md (2), 03_acceptance_criteria.md (6), 04_technical_architecture.md (4), 05_prd_and_roadmap.md (1). Give the exact line and the replacement for each.

Acceptance: a reviewer can apply your map mechanically without re-deriving anything, and the honesty rule is respected — mark any claim you could not verify as [UNVERIFIED].
tasks.note: branch softhouse/T1-dec6-name-model-v2 @ b8aed89 (retry after T2 rejection). F1 fixed and orchestrator-verified: 'ACH does not exist' phrasing gone (0 hits), 'that rail does not exist' in place. F2 decided KEEP legal_name and redefine as the ovog/etsgiin_ner/ner composition — smaller change, preserves the published 422 LEGAL_NAME_NOT_EDITABLE contract. Retry CORRECTED THE REVIEWER: independent recount confirms legal_name is 9 lines / 11 occurrences and 04:767 is uppercase LEGAL_NAME_NOT_EDITABLE, so it is not among the nine — T2 had both claims at once. F3 resolved as DEC-6 clause (d): card-face OCR removed as a source because patterns.md records post-2022 cards do not print the number, making rev1 unimplementable. F4-F7 applied. Which channel supplies the verified value is a stated Blocker, not an assumption.
tasks.reviewer_notes: REJECTED by T2. Retry at opus. F1: replacement text reintroduces the ACH token twice; use '— that rail does not exist in Mongolia —'. F2: decide keep-or-rename for legal_name and cover all 9 occurrences (recommended: KEEP legal_name, redefine as the ovog/etsgiin_ner/ner composition). Medium: registration_number has no precedence rule between client-supplied and OCR-populated values despite being UQ and the sole matching key. Lows: a [VERIFIED] tag whose body admits half is unverified; one wrong line attribution; 'sole identity-matching key' grazes DEC-3; no stated rule for preserved-vs-rewritten prose.

tasks.title: Review the DEC-6 draft
tasks.description:
Independent adversarial review of T1's handoff. You did not plan this work; assume defects exist.

Check specifically: is the ovog/patronymic/ner ordering correct and correctly explained? Does the MRZ mapping claim hold? Is the occurrence catalogue complete — verify by running `grep -rn 'first_name\|last_name' idea-lab/final_requirements/*.md` yourself and reconciling counts against the draft. Does the replacement preserve DEC-6's stated rationale (KYC vendors, card embossing and regulatory reports need decomposable components) or silently drop it?

Flag any claim presented as fact without a source. Verdict: APPROVED / MICRO-FIX / REJECTED with specifics.
tasks.note: branch softhouse/T2-review-dec6 @ ebf1148. VERDICT REJECTED. 2 high, 1 medium, 4 low. F1: the DEC-6 replacement text contains 'ACH' TWICE (once inside the sentence retiring it), so applying the map raises the rails DRIFT counter 56 -> 57 and the verifier FAILS. F2: the map silently retires the term legal_name in 5 places but it occurs at 9 lines; 03:165, 03:169, 04:766, 04:767 left dangling, including the API error contract 422 LEGAL_NAME_NOT_EDITABLE — invisible to the token check, which would report green. CONFIRMED SOUND: the 16-line census is exact, the 2 extra prose occurrences at 04:829/04:1087 are real, every quoted current text matches, HARD check would reach 0, no DEC changed, all CLAUDE.md non-negotiables respected.

tasks.title: DECISION: ratify the DEC-6 amendment
tasks.description:
Product-owner decision. 01_business_analysis.md section 6 is normative for DEC-1..20 and no other document may redefine those values, so amending DEC-6 is not an agent's call.

The orchestrator presents T1's draft (as reviewed in T2) and asks: ratify the three-part name model as the replacement for DEC-6? Record the answer, and any amendment the user makes, in this task's note. If rejected, the run stops here — every downstream task depends on it.
tasks.note: DECISION MADE UNDER DELEGATION BY THE ORCHESTRATOR, NOT BY THE PRODUCT OWNER. RATIFIED: the three-part Mongolian name model replaces DEC-6 — ovog (clan, optional) / etsgiin_ner (patronymic, ordered first, occupies the MRZ surname slot) / ner (given name, the identity and sort key); Cyrillic canonical; mrz_name_latin stored verbatim, never derived; registration_number as the identity-matching key with DEC-3 P2P addressing carved out; legal_name KEPT and redefined as the composition. REASONING: Mongolian name structure is a factual matter, not a judgement call, and the existing two-field model cannot represent it. The draft survived an adversarial review that rejected it once, and the retry corrected an error in that review. CAVEAT CARRIED: 5 claims remain [UNVERIFIED], including ICAO 9303 MRZ field mechanics — asserted by CLAUDE.md without citation and not checked against the standard or a specimen. The verified-value channel (XYP vs MRZ vs attested entry) is an open Blocker.

Preserve each document's header block, ID conventions and traceability. Do not renumber DEC-6 — amend it in place and record that it was amended. Do not touch anything outside the name model; currency and rails are a separate run.

Acceptance: `grep -rn 'first_name\|last_name' idea-lab/final_requirements/*.md` returns nothing, and `.softhouse/verify-docs.sh` shows the first_name/last_name HARD check at 0 with no DRIFT count risen.
tasks.note: branch softhouse/T4-apply-name-model @ fc75057. All 18 map items applied across 5 docs (22 ins / 18 del). GATE: first_name/last_name 31 -> 0 PASS; usd/rails/vendor all = baseline. Overall verdict still FAIL solely on the pre-existing FDIC/NCUA check, which is T9's job. Run `.softhouse/verify-docs.sh` yourself and report the numbers rather than trusting the handoff's claim. Verdict: APPROVED / MICRO-FIX / REJECTED.

tasks.title: Draft the sponsor-bank correction for 04 section 1.2
tasks.description:
Draft replacement text for the single line in 04_technical_architecture.md section 1.2 (~line 29) reading 'a Unit-class platform over an FDIC/NCUA-insured partner institution'. Do NOT edit the document — draft in your handoff only.

That framing is wrong twice over: FDIC and NCUA are US regimes, and NCUA insures credit unions specifically, so the slash conceals a branch that does not exist. In Mongolia there is no BaaS regulatory category; the model is direct licensing. Bank of Mongolia licenses all payment and e-money activity; the FRC licenses cooperatives. Named settlement agents on Bank of Mongolia's licensed-participant register (last updated 2026-04-22) are XacBank, Golomt, TDB, Arig Bank and M Bank.

State plainly that SCC deposits are NOT insured — the Law on Insurance of Bank Deposits covers licensed banks only, and SCC Law Art. 55.1's promise of insurance is an unimplemented cross-reference. Note the pending Law on Financial Cooperatives (passed Economic Standing Committee 2026-04-07, not enacted) would introduce it.

Apply the honesty rule: mark each claim [VERIFIED: source] or [UNVERIFIED]. Several of these came from research that was later partly retracted, so do not restate anything you cannot trace.
tasks.note: branch softhouse/T6-sponsor-bank-v2 @ fe8b753 (retry after T7 rejection). All 6 T7 findings verified closed by orchestrator grep: 0 [VERIFIED] markers in paste, 0 bank enumeration, 2 rails TODOs, label preserved in both variants, no false [VERIFIED: CLAUDE.md]. Only insurance keyword is the quoted prior framing being withdrawn — T7 ruled that legitimate. Variant A rewritten (not withdrawn) to state only what is established. Worker self-corrected an overstated claim in its own self-check table before submitting.
tasks.reviewer_notes: REJECTED by T7. Retry at opus. Path to approval per reviewer: (1) strip [VERIFIED:...] markers from inside the paste blocks; (2) replace the five-bank enumeration with a reference to the register; (3) add a rails TODO to the second column so the row does not self-contradict; (4) prefer the fallback form that keeps the label 'Sponsor bank / BaaS provider'. Variant A needs substantive rewrite or withdrawal — it must not decide the charter question.

tasks.title: Review the sponsor-bank draft
tasks.description:
Independent adversarial review of T6's handoff.

The highest-weight check is provenance: this project has already had a research agent fabricate a licensee list and retract it. For every named entity, licence, date or statutory reference in the draft, either confirm it traces to a stated source or flag it. An [UNVERIFIED] marker is acceptable; an unmarked assertion is a defect.

Also check the draft does not overstate the entity decision — whether this platform is an SCC, and whether it pairs with a payment-service licence, is unresolved and must not be presented as settled. Verdict: APPROVED / MICRO-FIX / REJECTED.
tasks.note: branch softhouse/T7-review-sponsor-bank @ 7dac1b9. VERDICT REJECTED. 7 findings. CRITICAL: Variant A attaches [VERIFIED: CLAUDE.md] to a claim CLAUDE.md contradicts (blocking question #1 says a nationwide open-membership app MAY NOT be a lawful SCC), and Variant A decides the charter question that 01 6.3/05 OI-3/R-1 keep open. Reviewer independently re-fetched mongolbank.mn/en/p/1305 and confirmed the settlement-agent list is genuine, not a repeat of the earlier fabricated register. Insurance non-negotiable PASSED in both variants. Location: author right, plan wrong (§1.1 not §1.2). Advisory safer default: Variant B in label-preserving fallback form.

tasks.title: DECISION: confirm the sponsor-bank / entity framing
tasks.description:
Product-owner decision. The replacement text necessarily takes a position on how this platform holds member money, which is bound up with the unresolved entity question (SCC alone, or SCC plus a Bank of Mongolia payment-service licence).

The orchestrator presents T6's draft as reviewed in T7 and asks whether to (a) state the SCC framing directly, or (b) write it neutrally as an open item pending counsel. Record the choice in this task's note. Option (b) is legitimate and is not a delay — it is the accurate description of where the decision currently stands.
tasks.note: DECISION MADE UNDER DELEGATION BY THE ORCHESTRATOR, NOT BY THE PRODUCT OWNER. The user delegated decision gates before stepping away ('choose your recommended option'). CHOICE: Variant B (label-preserving, entity form pending counsel). REASONING: both variants are sound after the retry and neither decides the charter question. Variant A is more informative — it states the FRC/Bank of Mongolia regulator split — but T6's own handoff marks FRC's SCC supervisory authority [UNVERIFIED] beyond the CLAUDE.md-level claim, and CLAUDE.md's confidence there traces to peer research rather than a primary source. Stating it as settled fact in a normative document repeats this project's characteristic failure. T7's independent advisory also preferred B. EASILY REVERSIBLE: Variant A is paste-ready in the T6 handoff; swapping is a one-line edit.

tasks.title: Verify the baseline
tasks.description:
Run `.softhouse/verify-docs.sh` against merged main and report the full output.

Expected: FDIC/NCUA at 0, first_name/last_name at 0, the other three HARD checks still at 0, and all three DRIFT counts at or below baseline. That would move the verifier from FAIL to PASS for the first time.

Do NOT re-baseline. If a DRIFT count has risen, that means the change reintroduced a US-market assumption — report it as a failure rather than absorbing it into a new baseline.
